comment_text.sn35.116:1.1: The “end of the world” is taught to brahmin cosmologists at AN 9.38, and illustrated with the parable of Rohitassa at SN 2.26 = AN 4.45. | I use both “world” and “cosmos” for loka. As this discourse shows, the exact meaning is highly mutable and context-specific.

root_text.sn35.116:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, gamanena lokassa antaṁ ñāteyyaṁ, daṭṭheyyaṁ, patteyyanti vadāmi.
root_text.sn35.116:1.2: Na ca panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, appatvā lokassa antaṁ dukkhassa antakiriyaṁ vadāmī”ti.
root_text.sn35.116:1.3: Idaṁ vatvā bhagavā uṭṭhāyāsanā vihāraṁ pāvisi.
root_text.sn35.116:1.4: Atha kho tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ acirapakkantassa bhagavato etadahosi:
root_text.sn35.116:1.5: “idaṁ kho no, āvuso, bhagavā saṅkhittena uddesaṁ uddisitvā vitthārena atthaṁ avibhajitvā uṭṭhāyāsanā vihāraṁ paviṭṭho:
root_text.sn35.116:1.6: ‘“nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, gamanena lokassa antaṁ ñāteyyaṁ, daṭṭheyyaṁ, patteyyan”ti vadāmi.
root_text.sn35.116:1.7: Na ca panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, appatvā lokassa antaṁ dukkhassa antakiriyaṁ vadāmī’ti.
root_text.sn35.116:1.8: Ko nu kho imassa bhagavatā saṅkhittena uddesassa uddiṭṭhassa vitthārena atthaṁ avibhattassa vitthārena atthaṁ vibhajeyyā”ti?
root_text.sn35.116:2.2: “ayaṁ kho āyasmā ānando satthu ceva saṁvaṇṇito, sambhāvito ca viññūnaṁ sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
root_text.sn35.116:2.3: Pahoti cāyasmā ānando imassa bhagavatā saṅkhittena uddesassa uddiṭṭhassa vitthārena atthaṁ avibhattassa vitthārena atthaṁ vibhajituṁ.
root_text.sn35.116:2.4: Yannūna mayaṁ yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkameyyāma; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etamatthaṁ paṭipuccheyyāmā”ti.
root_text.sn35.116:3.1: Atha kho te bhikkhū yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā ānandena saddhiṁ sammodiṁsu.
root_text.sn35.116:3.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn35.116:4.1: “Idaṁ kho no, āvuso ānanda, bhagavā saṅkhittena uddesaṁ uddisitvā vitthārena atthaṁ avibhajitvā uṭṭhāyāsanā vihāraṁ paviṭṭho:
root_text.sn35.116:4.2: ‘nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, gamanena lokassa antaṁ ñāteyyaṁ, daṭṭheyyaṁ, patteyyanti vadāmi.
root_text.sn35.116:4.3: Na ca panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, appatvā lokassa antaṁ dukkhassa antakiriyaṁ vadāmī’ti.
root_text.sn35.116:4.4: Tesaṁ no, āvuso, amhākaṁ acirapakkantassa bhagavato etadahosi:
root_text.sn35.116:4.5: ‘idaṁ kho no, āvuso, bhagavā saṅkhittena uddesaṁ uddisitvā vitthārena atthaṁ avibhajitvā uṭṭhāyāsanā vihāraṁ paviṭṭho—
root_text.sn35.116:4.6: nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, gamanena lokassa antaṁ ñāteyyaṁ, daṭṭheyyaṁ, patteyyanti vadāmi.
root_text.sn35.116:4.7: Na ca panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, appatvā lokassa antaṁ dukkhassa antakiriyaṁ vadāmīti.
root_text.sn35.116:4.8: Ko nu kho imassa bhagavatā saṅkhittena uddesassa uddiṭṭhassa vitthārena atthaṁ avibhattassa vitthārena atthaṁ vibhajeyyā’ti?
root_text.sn35.116:4.10: ‘ayaṁ kho, āvuso, āyasmā ānando satthu ceva saṁvaṇṇito, sambhāvito ca viññūnaṁ sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
root_text.sn35.116:4.11: Pahoti cāyasmā ānando imassa bhagavatā saṅkhittena uddesassa uddiṭṭhassa vitthārena atthaṁ avibhattassa vitthārena atthaṁ vibhajituṁ.
root_text.sn35.116:4.12: Yannūna mayaṁ yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkameyyāma; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etamatthaṁ paṭipuccheyyāmā’ti.
root_text.sn35.116:5.1: “Seyyathāpi, āvuso, puriso sāratthiko sāragavesī sārapariyesanaṁ caramāno mahato rukkhassa tiṭṭhato sāravato atikkammeva, mūlaṁ atikkammeva, khandhaṁ sākhāpalāse sāraṁ pariyesitabbaṁ maññeyya;
root_text.sn35.116:5.2: evaṁ sampadamidaṁ āyasmantānaṁ satthari sammukhībhūte taṁ bhagavantaṁ atisitvā amhe etamatthaṁ paṭipucchitabbaṁ maññatha.
root_text.sn35.116:5.3: So hāvuso, bhagavā jānaṁ jānāti, passaṁ passati—
root_text.sn35.116:5.4: cakkhubhūto, ñāṇabhūto, dhammabhūto, brahmabhūto, vattā, pavattā, atthassa ninnetā, amatassa dātā, dhammassāmī, tathāgato.
root_text.sn35.116:5.5: So ceva panetassa kālo ahosi yaṁ bhagavantaṁyeva etamatthaṁ paṭipuccheyyātha.
root_text.sn35.116:5.6: Yathā vo bhagavā byākareyya tathā vo dhāreyyāthā”ti.
root_text.sn35.116:6.1: “Addhāvuso ānanda, bhagavā jānaṁ jānāti, passaṁ passati—
root_text.sn35.116:6.2: cakkhubhūto, ñāṇabhūto, dhammabhūto, brahmabhūto, vattā, pavattā, atthassa ninnetā, amatassa dātā, dhammassāmī, tathāgato.
root_text.sn35.116:6.3: So ceva panetassa kālo ahosi yaṁ bhagavantaṁyeva etamatthaṁ paṭipuccheyyāma.
root_text.sn35.116:6.4: Yathā no bhagavā byākareyya tathā naṁ dhāreyyāma.
root_text.sn35.116:6.5: Api cāyasmā ānando satthu ceva saṁvaṇṇito, sambhāvito ca viññūnaṁ sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
root_text.sn35.116:6.6: Pahoti cāyasmā ānando imassa bhagavatā saṅkhittena uddesassa uddiṭṭhassa vitthārena atthaṁ avibhattassa vitthārena atthaṁ vibhajituṁ.
root_text.sn35.116:7.2: “Evamāvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato ānandassa paccassosuṁ.
root_text.sn35.116:8.1: “Yaṁ kho vo, āvuso, bhagavā saṅkhittena uddesaṁ uddisitvā vitthārena atthaṁ avibhajitvā uṭṭhāyāsanā vihāraṁ paviṭṭho:
root_text.sn35.116:8.2: ‘nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, gamanena lokassa antaṁ ñāteyyaṁ, daṭṭheyyaṁ, patteyyanti vadāmi.
root_text.sn35.116:8.3: Na ca panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, appatvā lokassa antaṁ dukkhassa antakiriyaṁ vadāmī’ti,
root_text.sn35.116:8.4: imassa khvāhaṁ, āvuso, bhagavatā saṅkhittena uddesassa uddiṭṭhassa vitthārena atthaṁ avibhattassa vitthārena atthaṁ ājānāmi.
root_text.sn35.116:8.5: Yena kho, āvuso, lokasmiṁ lokasaññī hoti lokamānī—
root_text.sn35.116:8.7: Kena cāvuso, lokasmiṁ lokasaññī hoti lokamānī?
root_text.sn35.116:8.8: Cakkhunā kho, āvuso, lokasmiṁ lokasaññī hoti lokamānī.
root_text.sn35.116:8.11: jivhāya kho, āvuso, lokasmiṁ lokasaññī hoti lokamānī.
root_text.sn35.116:8.13: manena kho, āvuso, lokasmiṁ lokasaññī hoti lokamānī.
root_text.sn35.116:8.14: Yena kho, āvuso, lokasmiṁ lokasaññī hoti lokamānī—
root_text.sn35.116:8.16: Yaṁ kho vo, āvuso, bhagavā saṅkhittena uddesaṁ uddisitvā vitthārena atthaṁ avibhajitvā uṭṭhāyāsanā vihāraṁ paviṭṭho:
root_text.sn35.116:8.17: ‘nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, gamanena lokassa antaṁ ñāteyyaṁ, daṭṭheyyaṁ, patteyyanti vadāmi.
root_text.sn35.116:8.18: Na ca panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, appatvā lokassa antaṁ dukkhassa antakiriyaṁ vadāmī’ti,
root_text.sn35.116:8.19: imassa khvāhaṁ, āvuso, bhagavatā saṅkhittena uddesassa uddiṭṭhassa vitthārena atthaṁ avibhattassa evaṁ vitthārena atthaṁ ājānāmi.
root_text.sn35.116:8.20: Ākaṅkhamānā ca pana tumhe āyasmanto bhagavantaṁyeva upasaṅkamitvā etamatthaṁ paṭipuccheyyātha.
root_text.sn35.116:8.21: Yathā vo bhagavā byākaroti tathā naṁ dhāreyyāthā”ti.
root_text.sn35.116:9.1: “Evamāvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato ānandassa paṭissutvā uṭṭhāyāsanā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn35.116:10.1: “Yaṁ kho no, bhante, bhagavā saṅkhittena uddesaṁ uddisitvā vitthārena atthaṁ avibhajitvā uṭṭhāyāsanā vihāraṁ paviṭṭho:
root_text.sn35.116:10.2: ‘nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, gamanena lokassa antaṁ ñāteyyaṁ, daṭṭheyyaṁ, patteyyanti vadāmi.
root_text.sn35.116:10.3: Na ca panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, appatvā lokassa antaṁ dukkhassa antakiriyaṁ vadāmī’ti.
root_text.sn35.116:10.4: Tesaṁ no, bhante, amhākaṁ acirapakkantassa bhagavato etadahosi:
root_text.sn35.116:10.5: ‘idaṁ kho no, āvuso, bhagavā saṅkhittena uddesaṁ uddisitvā vitthārena atthaṁ avibhajitvā uṭṭhāyāsanā vihāraṁ paviṭṭho—
root_text.sn35.116:10.6: nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, gamanena lokassa antaṁ ñāteyyaṁ, daṭṭheyyaṁ, patteyyanti vadāmi.
root_text.sn35.116:10.7: Na ca panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, appatvā lokassa antaṁ dukkhassa antakiriyaṁ vadāmīti.
root_text.sn35.116:10.8: Ko nu kho imassa bhagavatā saṅkhittena uddesassa uddiṭṭhassa vitthārena atthaṁ avibhattassa vitthārena atthaṁ vibhajeyyā’ti?
root_text.sn35.116:10.10: ‘ayaṁ kho āyasmā ānando satthu ceva saṁvaṇṇito, sambhāvito ca viññūnaṁ sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
root_text.sn35.116:10.11: Pahoti cāyasmā ānando imassa bhagavatā saṅkhittena uddesassa uddiṭṭhassa vitthārena atthaṁ avibhattassa vitthārena atthaṁ vibhajituṁ.
root_text.sn35.116:10.12: Yannūna mayaṁ yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkameyyāma; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etamatthaṁ paṭipuccheyyāmā’ti.
root_text.sn35.116:10.13: Atha kho mayaṁ, bhante, yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkamimha; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etamatthaṁ paṭipucchimha.
root_text.sn35.116:10.14: Tesaṁ no, bhante, āyasmatā ānandena imehi ākārehi imehi padehi imehi byañjanehi attho vibhatto”ti.
root_text.sn35.116:11.2: Mañcepi tumhe, bhikkhave, etamatthaṁ paṭipuccheyyātha, ahampi taṁ evamevaṁ byākareyyaṁ yathā taṁ ānandena byākataṁ.
root_text.sn35.116:11.3: Eso cevetassa attho, evañca naṁ dhāreyyāthā”ti.

translation_text.sn35.116:0.2: 12. The World and the Kinds of Sensual Stimulation
translation_text.sn35.116:0.3: Traveling to the End of the World
translation_text.sn35.116:1.1: “Mendicants, I say it’s not possible to know or see or reach the end of the world by traveling.
translation_text.sn35.116:1.2: But I also say there’s no making an end of suffering without reaching the end of the world.”
translation_text.sn35.116:1.3: When he had spoken, the Blessed One got up from his seat and entered his dwelling.
translation_text.sn35.116:1.4: Soon after the Buddha left, those mendicants considered,
translation_text.sn35.116:1.5: “The Buddha gave this brief summary recital, then entered his dwelling without explaining the meaning in detail. …
translation_text.sn35.116:1.8: Who can explain in detail the meaning of this brief summary recital given by the Buddha?”
translation_text.sn35.116:2.2: “This Venerable Ānanda is praised by the Buddha and esteemed by his sensible spiritual companions.
translation_text.sn35.116:2.3: He is capable of explaining in detail the meaning of this brief summary recital given by the Buddha.
translation_text.sn35.116:2.4: Let’s go to him, and ask him about this matter.”
translation_text.sn35.116:3.1: Then those mendicants went to Ānanda and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn35.116:3.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, they sat down to one side. They told him what had happened, and said,
translation_text.sn35.116:5.1: “Reverends, suppose there was a person in need of heartwood. And while wandering in search of heartwood he’d come across a large tree standing with heartwood. But he’d pass over the roots and trunk, imagining that the heartwood should be sought in the branches and leaves.
translation_text.sn35.116:5.2: Such is the consequence for the venerables. Though you were face to face with the Buddha, you bypassed him, imagining that you should ask me about this matter.
translation_text.sn35.116:5.3: For he is the Buddha, the one who knows and sees. He is vision, he is knowledge, he is the manifestation of principle, he is the manifestation of divinity. He is the teacher, the proclaimer, the elucidator of meaning, the bestower of freedom from death, the lord of truth, the Realized One.
translation_text.sn35.116:5.5: That was the time to approach the Buddha and ask about this matter.
translation_text.sn35.116:5.6: You should have remembered it in line with the Buddha’s answer.”
translation_text.sn35.116:6.1: “Certainly he is the Buddha, the one who knows and sees. He is vision, he is knowledge, he is the manifestation of principle, he is the manifestation of divinity. He is the teacher, the proclaimer, the elucidator of meaning, the bestower of freedom from death, the lord of truth, the Realized One.
translation_text.sn35.116:6.3: That was the time to approach the Buddha and ask about this matter.
translation_text.sn35.116:6.4: We should have remembered it in line with the Buddha’s answer.
translation_text.sn35.116:6.5: Still, Venerable Ānanda is praised by the Buddha and esteemed by his sensible spiritual companions.
translation_text.sn35.116:6.6: You are capable of explaining in detail the meaning of this brief summary recital given by the Buddha.
translation_text.sn35.116:6.7: Please explain this, if it’s no trouble.”
translation_text.sn35.116:7.1: “Then listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.sn35.116:8.1: “Reverends, the Buddha gave this brief summary recital, then entered his dwelling without explaining the meaning in detail:
translation_text.sn35.116:8.2: ‘Mendicants, I say it’s not possible to know or see or reach the end of the world by traveling.
translation_text.sn35.116:8.3: But I also say there’s no making an end of suffering without reaching the end of the world.’
translation_text.sn35.116:8.4: This is how I understand the detailed meaning of this summary recital.
translation_text.sn35.116:8.5: Whatever in the world through which you perceive the world and conceive the world
translation_text.sn35.116:8.6: is called the world in the training of the Noble One.
translation_text.sn35.116:8.7: And through what in the world do you perceive the world and conceive the world?
translation_text.sn35.116:8.8: Through the eye in the world you perceive the world and conceive the world.
translation_text.sn35.116:8.13: mind in the world you perceive the world and conceive the world.
translation_text.sn35.116:8.14: Whatever in the world through which you perceive the world and conceive the world
translation_text.sn35.116:8.15: is called the world in the training of the Noble One.
translation_text.sn35.116:8.16: When the Buddha gave this brief summary recital, then entered his dwelling without explaining the meaning in detail:
translation_text.sn35.116:8.17: ‘Mendicants, I say it’s not possible to know or see or reach the end of the world by traveling.
translation_text.sn35.116:8.18: But I also say there’s no making an end of suffering without reaching the end of the world.’
translation_text.sn35.116:8.19: That is how I understand the detailed meaning of this summary.
translation_text.sn35.116:8.20: If you wish, you may go to the Buddha and ask him about this.
translation_text.sn35.116:8.21: You should remember it in line with the Buddha’s answer.”
translation_text.sn35.116:9.1: “Yes, reverend,” replied those mendicants. Then they rose from their seats and went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened.
translation_text.sn35.116:10.14: Then they said, “And Ānanda explained the meaning to us in this manner, with these words and phrases.”
translation_text.sn35.116:11.1: “Mendicants, Ānanda is astute, he has great wisdom.
translation_text.sn35.116:11.2: If you came to me and asked this question, I would answer it in exactly the same way as Ānanda.
translation_text.sn35.116:11.3: That is what it means, and that’s how you should remember it.”
